commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer merge judgment for a protected, draft, compatibility-sensitive PR plus final proof/overlap coordination, not a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found in the sampled diff; the security-sensitive parts are hardening credential/provider/tool boundaries rather than adding dependencies, workflows, lockfile... commands.review_summary:
The branch adds broad runtime, plugin, provider, Codex app-server, channel, and outbound metadata guards plus regression tests so malformed or unreadable synthetic plugin/config/tool data is quarantined, omitted, diagnosed, or failed closed instead of raw-crashing.

Reproducibility: yes. at source/proof level: the PR body gives red/green focused repros for unreadable synthetic plugin/tool/provider metadata and the sampled current-main paths use direct property reads or JSON/stringification that this branch replaces with guarded readers. I did not rerun tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer merge judgment for a protected, draft, compatibility-sensitive PR plus final proof/overlap coordination, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(plugin): harden schema and metadata fuzz boundaries
target.body:
## Summary

- Hardened runtime/plugin schema and metadata boundaries so malformed or unreadable synthetic plugin data is quarantined, omitted, diagnosed, audited, or treated as absent instead of crashing assistant startup, doctor/setup flows, provider selection, channel authorization, Codex app-server routing, gateway startup planning, native approval routing, SDK helper APIs, tool policy filtering/audit, provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, embedded-agent provider schema diagnostics, plugin runtime tool factory resolution, central runtime schema projection, effective tool inventory, embedded system-prompt rendering, system-prompt diagnostics, or outbound message action handling.
- Kept tests synthetic: current fixtures use names like `fuzzplugin`, `mockplugin`, and `fuzz_move_*`, not the reported third-party plugin name.
- Latest slices guard central runtime schema projection, effective tool inventory, tool policy filtering/audit, embedded system-prompt construction, system-prompt report construction, provider tool descriptors, embedded-agent provider tool diagnostics, and plugin factory-returned tool descriptors. Unreadable `name`, unreadable `execute`, unreadable `parameters`, normalize/inspect hook throws, unreadable thrown values, unreadable diagnostic `violations`, and unreadable tool names now omit, diagnose, audit, or log bounded warnings instead of raw-crashing schema normalization/diagnostics/tool loading/inventory/reporting.
- Earlier slices harden outbound structured attachment params, outbound JSON params (`presentation`, `delivery`, `interactive`), `runMessageAction` input param copying, exported message action normalization/target-spec helpers, exported message action threading controls/writes, native approval routing, provider wizard metadata, manifest/channel metadata, Codex image payload handling, and embedded-agent schema quarantine.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas and hostile plugin/config/provider/manifest/native-approval/outbound-action metadata can crash or widen decisions before normal OpenClaw diagnostics, setup, provider routing, gateway startup planning, approval forwarding, message action validation, tool policy filtering/audit, provider tool-schema compatibility traversal, embedded-agent schema diagnostics, plugin runtime tool resolution, central runtime schema projection, embedded system-prompt rendering, system-prompt report construction, or Codex app-server handling complete.

Real environment tested: local focused Vitest through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`; broad remote validation on direct AWS Crabbox Linux runners because Blacksmith Testbox auth was unavailable in this shell.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for embedded system-prompt construction with an unreadable synthetic tool descriptor `name` while preserving a healthy `sessions_spawn` sibling.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for central runtime schema projection with unreadable synthetic tool descriptor fields (`name`, `parameters`).
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for effective tool inventory with unreadable synthetic tool descriptor fields (`name`, `parameters`) while preserving a healthy `exec` sibling.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for tool policy filtering and audit with unreadable synthetic core/plugin tool names while preserving a healthy `exec` sibling and auditing malformed drops as `tool[index]`.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for system-prompt report construction with unreadable synthetic tool descriptor fields (`name`, `parameters`) while preserving a healthy `exec` sibling.
- Direct red/green focused Vitest repro for plugin factory-returned tools with unreadable synthetic descriptor fields (`name`, `execute`, `parameters`).
- Direct pre/post `tsx` repros for embedded-agent provider normalize hook throws, unreadable provider diagnostics, unreadable synthetic provider tool descriptors, message action threading writes, unreadable threading controls, message action normalization params, runner params, provider wizard metadata, manifest channel metadata, native approval approver iterators, shared action params, structured attachments, and JSON action params.
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-embedded-system-prompt-post-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/system-prompt.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-tool-policy-pipeline-post-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-policy-pipeline.test.ts src/agents/tool-policy.test.ts src/agents/tool-policy.plugin-only-allowlist.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-system-prompt-report-post-rebase-2dcc node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/system-prompt-report.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-tools-effective-inventory-post-rebase node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools-effective-inventory.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-tool-schema-projection-c009 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `CI=true OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.tmp/vitest-active-tool-schema-warnings-c009 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor/shared/active-tool-schema-warnings.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.threading.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- Earlier focused batches for outbound normalization/params, shared action params, provider auth/onboarding, manifest/tool availability, channel/config fixtures, Codex image payloads, and fuzz guard type/lint drift.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check` and `node_modules/.bin/oxlint` on the latest touched file sets.
- `git diff --check`
- Added-line scrub for reported plugin names, local paths, and private network literals.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base refs/remotes/origin/main`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Evidence after fix:

- Latest embedded system-prompt slice proof after rebase to `c8cc010e09`: `system-prompt.test.ts` passed 2 Vitest projects / 18 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed with no accepted/actionable findings.
- Tool policy descriptor slice proof after rebase to `47e86bc1ac`: `tool-policy-pipeline.test.ts`, `tool-policy.test.ts`, and `tool-policy.plugin-only-allowlist.test.ts` passed 4 Vitest projects / 67 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed after addressing the audit under-count finding.
- System-prompt report slice proof after rebase to `2dcca3ec8a`: `system-prompt-report.test.ts` passed 1 file / 11 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed.
- Effective inventory slice proof after rebase to `60392a1136`: `tools-effective-inventory.test.ts` passed 2 Vitest projects / 38 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed.
- Central schema projection slice proof after rebase to `c0094a232d`: `tool-schema-projection.test.ts` passed 1 file / 7 tests; `active-tool-schema-warnings.test.ts` passed 1 file / 6 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and added-line scrub passed.
- Plugin runtime tool descriptor slice proof: `tools.optional.test.ts` passed 1 file / 62 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed.
- Embedded-agent provider diagnostics slice proof: `tool-schema-runtime.test.ts` passed 2 files / 12 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed after fixing the guarded `formatError` fallback.
- Provider-tool descriptor slice proof: `provider-tools.test.ts` passed 1 file / 15 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed.
- Message threading writes slice proof: `message-action-runner.threading.test.ts` passed 1 file / 24 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, `git diff --check`, added-line scrub, and local autoreview passed.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate after the provider-wizard slice: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_991a8c29acdf`, run `run_41811346ac17`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 on base `8e806e9125`.
- Earlier AWS Crabbox changed gate for the branch: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_52b1fe398bd3`, run `run_c85914033aaa`, command `pnpm check:changed`, exit 0 over core, coreTests, extensions, and extensionTests.
- Current branch head after rebase/push: `7c2e0c0b5a493c69f34cd80146350edd06cd5c4f`.
- Latest rebase/proof base: `c8cc010e09b98bdca4986145a62d003be851d2f02`.
- GitHub `main` advanced again after the latest rebase/proof/push to `9dd3bce549b4c2aad916cc9c1050225661f0cb9d`; rebase is pending for the next work slice.
- Current added-line scrub: no reported plugin name, local path, or private network literal in the branch diff.

Observed result after fix:

- Hostile synthetic config/schema/provider/manifest/native-approval/message-action/plugin-tool inputs are bounded at the relevant owner boundary instead of throwing raw getter/enumeration/serialization/spread errors.
- Embedded system-prompt rendering omits unreadable prompt-only tool names while preserving healthy sibling tool names.
- Central runtime schema projection reports unreadable tool descriptor fields as diagnostics and filters only affected tools, preserving healthy siblings.
- Effective tool inventory uses readable tool names for lookup and lets schema projection quarantine unreadable descriptors while preserving healthy sibling inventory entries.
- Tool policy filtering and plugin group expansion drop unreadable-name descriptors without raw-crashing, and audit logs include `tool[index]` placeholders so malformed drops remain counted.
- System-prompt report construction treats unreadable tool descriptor fields as absent, uses stable `tool[index]` fallback names for unreadable names, and still reports healthy sibling tools.
- Plugin factory-returned tools with unreadable descriptor fields are diagnosed as malformed and skipped while readable sibling tools continue to resolve.
- Embedded-agent provider schema normalization now keeps original tools when provider hooks throw, and diagnostics logging stays bounded when inspect hooks or diagnostic/tool metadata are hostile.
- Provider tool-schema compatibility traversal now guards descriptor reads and normalized descriptor copies before inspecting provider-specific schema keywords.
- Message action normalization, threading controls, threading writes, JSON params, structured attachments, and runner param copies now fail closed at the relevant boundary instead of raw-crashing on synthetic accessors.
- Provider wizard setup choices, gateway startup manifest planning, native approval routing, manifest tool availability, Codex image payload handling, and embedded-agent schema-quarantine fixtures retain their previously proven behavior.

What was not tested:

- Blacksmith Testbox was not run because `blacksmith auth login` is required in this shell; direct AWS Crabbox was used for broad remote gates.
- The latest broad Crabbox gate predates the newest outbound runner/normalization/threading/provider-tool/runtime-diagnostics/plugin-tool-descriptor/projection-descriptor/effective-inventory/system-prompt-report/tool-policy/embedded-system-prompt slices; those slices have focused local proof and local autoreview.
- GitHub target `main` advanced to `9dd3bce549` after the latest rebase/proof/push; no proof has been rerun against that newer tip yet.
- GitHub PR checks are queued/running on the pushed head.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No new config surface. Existing malformed/unreadable config, manifest, provider, tool descriptor, diagnostic, or action inputs now fail closed or resolve as absent at bounded reads.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed or unreadable tool/provider/plugin/native-approval/action metadata is rejected, omitted, or quarantined earlier instead of crashing runtime paths or broadening access.

What is the highest-risk area?

Overly strict sanitation could omit a valid provider tool descriptor, plugin tool descriptor, diagnostic payload, runtime projection descriptor, prompt-only tool name, or outbound action parameter.

How is that risk mitigated?

The changes preserve readable sibling entries, fail closed only for route/control/identity/idempotency-style action params in the newest outbound slices, keep unreadable payload-only fields as absent, preserve numeric alias behavior, and use focused regressions plus broad AWS `check:changed` gates from earlier branch slices.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review the draft PR at head `7c2e0c0b5a493c69f34cd80146350edd06cd5c4f`, or rebase once more if reviewer/CI requires the current `main` tip.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI is queued/running after the force-push. No local actionable autoreview findings remain. GitHub target `main` advanced after this push/readback, so the next work slice should start by rebasing from `9dd3bce549` or newer.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview findings were addressed, including Codex URL-backed image-block preservation, provider wizard receiver handling, native approval false-positive target matching, shared param-reader silent truncation, message action `topLevel`/`idempotencyKey` fail-closed handling, numeric legacy-target preservation, guarded message action threading controls, guarded message action threading writes, guarded provider tool descriptors, guarded embedded-agent provider diagnostics, guarded plugin runtime tool descriptors, guarded central schema projection descriptors, guarded effective inventory name lookup, guarded system-prompt report descriptor reads, accurate audit counting for malformed tool-policy drops, and guarded embedded system-prompt tool names.
target.author: vincentkoc
